title.en: MEASUREMENT OF MECHANICAL VIBRATIONS OR ULTRASONIC, SONIC OR INFRASONIC WAVES
Measuring {characteristics of} vibrations in solids by using direct conduction to the detector (G01H9/00, G01H11/00 take precedence)
{of rotating machines (G01H1/10 takes precedence)}
of vibrations which are transverse to direction of propagation
of torsional vibrations
of longitudinal or not specified vibrations
Measuring {characteristics of} vibrations by using a detector in a fluid (G01H7/00, G01H9/00, G01H11/00 take precedence)
{Testing or calibrating of detectors covered by the subgroups of G01H3/00 (calibrating geophysical instruments, e.g. seismic receivers G01V13/00)}
Frequency
Amplitude; Power
Measuring propagation velocity of ultrasonic, sonic or infrasonic waves {, e.g. of pressure waves}
Measuring reverberation time {; room acoustic measurements}
Measuring mechanical vibrations or ultrasonic, sonic or infrasonic waves by using radiation-sensitive means, e.g. optical means
{for representing acoustic field distribution (sonar systems for imaging G01S7/56, G01S15/89; acoustic holography G03H3/00)}
{using fibre optic sensors (light guides per se G02B6/00, acousto-optical devices specially adapted for gating or modulating in optical wave guides G02F1/125)}
{by using ultrasonic waves (measuring position using ultrasonic waves G01S15/02)}
Measuring mechanical vibrations or ultrasonic, sonic or infrasonic waves by detecting changes in electric or magnetic properties
by magnetic means, e.g. reluctance
by electric means
Measuring resonant frequency
Measuring mechanical or acoustic impedance
Measuring mechanical vibrations or ultrasonic, sonic or infrasonic waves, not provided for in the other groups of this subclass
